verdict_summary: The three designs agree on the load-bearing kernel (hookio three-tier contract, single-source gate config + hardcoded FLOOR, direct invocation, manifest installer, merge-never-clobber, dual channels, selftest-as-acceptance, canary job, skip-local/fail-closed-CI asymmetry, AUTH stub with production fatal, catalog-gated plain-SQL RLS suite) — that consensus is sound and should ship as-is. They diverge materially on 12 points: what Rust work runs in the Stop chain, whether licenses/prompts/version-sync are default gates, isolation/specta/React-Compiler default-vs-module, default template CI weight, FLOOR membership of project-shaped gates, Windows CI cadence, Entra placeholder handling, retrofit merge scope, write-guard posture on Tauri config surfaces, CLAUDE.md vs AGENTS.md, and latency claims. Adjudications mostly favor B's gate content (research-backed cheap gates), C's delivery mechanics (rust stamp, apt list, windows-every-PR selftest, root-only retrofit merge, runtime-env Entra), and A's middle-weight defaults (mock-IPC a11y lane, screens/aliveness gates, fallback contingencies). Each design has at least one real infeasibility (A: bootstrap rust-skip assumption + specta-in-contracts-step; B: JSON tokens in the provenance regex + blanket Cargo.toml/capabilities protection + one-job bootstrap; C: isolation-module contradiction with the tauri-policy gate), and all three miss a dozen research findings — most importantly the pgvector extversion assert, the HITL state-machine/disjointness gates for the consuming product, the Zod single-instance gate, and the keyboard-registry WCAG 2.1.4 unit test. The merged plan below resolves every divergence with a single decision.
contradictions: Rust in the Stop chain — A runs cargo fmt + full clippy --workspace -D warnings (dirty-or-CI gated); B runs fmt + cargo check every turn via run-if; C runs cargo check only, gated by a content-hash stamp. B/C are right: research T3-r4 explicitly places clippy -D warnings in CI/pre-push and only 'cargo check (fast) when src-tauri changed' in the Stop chain; A's clippy contradicts its own latency budget (10–30s warm, minutes cold). MERGED: cargo fmt --check (near-free) + cargo check --locked behind C's content-hash stamp (.harness/rust-check.ok over src-tauri/** + Cargo.lock — stronger than A's git-dirty detection, which misses committed-but-never-checked states); stamp ignored and fail-closed when CI=true; clippy/nextest/cargo-deny in the CI rust lane only.

License gate placement — B: default validate step (pnpm licenses list --json --prod vs allowlist, ~2s); A: shipped-CI-only (licenses.yml); C: ci-provenance module. B is right: research T12-r1 ENF says wire it into the validate chain so the Stop gate itself refuses on a violation; it is fast, deterministic, and bootstrap-green with a curated allowlist + reviewed-exceptions map. cargo-deny licenses stays CI (needs cargo); NOTICES generation stays in the provenance/release module (all agree).

Isolation pattern + tauri-specta — A/B default, C modules (tauri-isolation, tauri-specta). Research T1-r5 (assert pattern.use=='isolation' in CI) and T1-r3 (committed specta bindings + drift gate) support default. MERGED: both default, with A's recorded contingency (if isolation destabilizes selftest on a Tauri bump, relax the policy assert to warn + promote to strict tier via ADR) and the specta drift check moved out of the fast contracts step (see infeasibilities) into the stamp-gated rust step + CI. C's ESLint src/ipc-only invoke rule ships regardless as belt.

React Compiler — B default (exact-pinned) vs C module (ui-react-compiler) vs A silent. B is right: research T7-r5 recommends adoption with an exact pin, and a fresh scaffold is the safest adoption point (zero latent Rules-of-React violations; react-hooks recommended-latest enforced from day one). C's 'behavior-changing on day one' risk is precisely backwards — retrofitting the compiler onto the mature med-school codebase is the risky path. check-version-sync asserts the exact pin (no ^/~).

Default template CI weight — B ships browser-mode lane + testcontainers integration lane + coverage ratchet + oasdiff in default quality-gate; C ships the minimum (a11y floor = jsx-a11y lint only); A ships a middle (Playwright mock-IPC fast lane + degraded-network). A's middle wins, tilted by the user's WCAG 2.2 AA keyboard-first goal: C's lint-only a11y floor is too weak (axe+jsx-a11y ≈ 30–57% of WCAG per T3-P6/T7-P1, and lint alone is less than that); B's browser-mode + testcontainers lanes add flake/cost to every consuming PR. MERGED default CI: min-floor, unit, runtime-RLS, rust lane, Playwright fast lane (mock-IPC + axe wcag22aa + keyboard smoke + degraded-network), migration-safety, api-contract (regen-diff + oasdiff-vs-main, which is meaningful from day one — B right), licenses(cargo), codeql/osv/gitleaks-binary/actions-lint; browser-mode component scans and testcontainers lanes move into gate-a11y-deep / integration modules; coverage ratchet default-CI-only with scaffold-calibrated floors via a bot-PR flow (B right on the ratchet, wrong on auto-commit-to-main).

FLOOR membership of contracts/schema-rls/migrations — A/B include them in FLOOR; C excludes them as 'project-shaped self-skips' (mirroring the sibling's exclusion of route-boundaries/aliveness). MERGED: include them in FLOOR for tamper evidence, and put the shape-awareness INSIDE each gate script (print a loud SKIP when packages/schema or apps/server is absent, fail closed in CI when present). The sibling excluded project-shaped gates only because those scripts had no self-skip; with self-skip built in, floor membership and retrofit tolerance are compatible. Plus adopt A's selftest assertion that every FLOOR name exists in VALIDATE_STEPS with an identical command string (verified against the real validate.mjs mechanism: merge is by name, drift means CI runs both).

Windows CI cadence — C runs bootstrap-windows (pnpm validate) on every PR; A/B path-filter + nightly. C is right for the harness repo: public-repo Actions minutes are free (the 2x billing argument applies only to private consuming repos) and Windows-first is the product promise. A/B are right for the SHIPPED template CI (path-filtered windows job + nightly, enforced by a workflow-lint rule). MERGED: harness selftest = windows validate+vitest every PR; tauri-build-smoke (full NSIS bundle + /S install/uninstall/registry asserts, C's lane) path-filtered + nightly (nightly doubles as the rust-cache warmer against 7-day eviction).

Entra IDs: placeholders vs runtime env — A/B register ENTRA_TENANT_ID/CLIENT_ID (A also API_AUDIENCE) as installer placeholders; C makes them runtime env in env.example. C is right: tenant/client IDs are per-environment deployment config, placeholder GUIDs baked into committed files invite real IDs being committed and add hygiene noise, and AUTH_MODE=stub default means no build ever needs them. Exception: API_ORIGIN must be a placeholder (B right) because it feeds the committed tauri.conf.json CSP connect-src, which check-tauri-policy asserts. Final placeholder set: the 5 carried + PRODUCT_IDENTIFIER (≤30-char lint), WINDOWS_PUBLISHER, API_ORIGIN, DB_NAME.

Retrofit merge scope — A/B merge package.json per-workspace (+ A adds merge-workspace-yaml.mjs); C merges root only, seeds workspaces additively. MERGED: C's root-only package.json merge (gates are direct-invoked from the repo root, never via workspace scripts, so root merge suffices for wiring) PLUS A's merge-workspace-yaml.mjs (packages-glob union + catalog add-missing/never-downgrade — without it retrofit cannot register apps/* or pin the catalog). Per-workspace package.json merging is deferred with documented scope; C's honesty beats A/B's untested matrix at v0.1.

Write-guard posture on tauri.conf.json/capabilities/Cargo.toml — B blanket-PROTECTs them (any edit needs HARNESS_ALLOW_SELF_EDIT); A/C allow edits but content-check specific weakenings. A/C are right: adding a permission or crate dep is routine vertical-slice work; blanket protection trains humans to leave the escape hatch set, which hollows tamper evidence everywhere. check-tauri-policy (structural JSON parse) is the authority; write-guard regexes are the fast tripwire, and every deny message names the structural gate. Keep identity.lock.json blanket-protected (all agree), content-check Cargo.toml (deny removing [lints] workspace=true, deny adding tauri-plugin-shell/tauri-plugin-http), and adopt B/C's append-only migrations rule (edits to existing drizzle/*.sql denied, new files allowed).

CLAUDE.md kernel vs AGENTS.md canonical — B adopts AGENTS.md with CLAUDE.md='@AGENTS.md' + a CI equality check + docs-commands-verify (research T9-r0, tldraw pattern); A/C carry the sibling's CLAUDE.md kernel. Adopt B: the parity mandate covers enforcement architecture, not file naming; the indirection is one line, tool-agnostic, and the docs-commands-verify job (fenced commands must exist) is a real anti-rot gate none of the sibling's docs have.

Stop-chain latency claims — A says 90–150s warm, B ≤45s, C 45–90s, for nearly identical work; they cannot all describe the same scaffold, and B's ≤45s with 16 steps is the least credible. Verified: the sibling wires the Stop hook with timeout: 600, so all three fit the mechanism. MERGED: adopt no aspirational number — measure warm wall time in selftest and assert a generous budget (≤120s scaffold-warm, warn at 90) per A/C's mechanism; biome-first ordering; keep clippy, browser mode, WDIO, mutation, and live evals out of the chain (all three agree, and T3-P7 is explicit that slow chains get gamed).
infeasibilities: A's ubuntu bootstrap job assumes 'rust step SKIPs (cargo absent / src-tauri clean)' — this breaks either way: if the runner image ships cargo the step RUNS, and cargo check on Tauri's dep tree on Linux hard-requires the pinned webkit apt packages (libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev, libappindicator3-dev, librsvg2-dev, patchelf) that A never installs, plus minutes of cold compile; if cargo is absent, A's own fail-closed rule (CI=true ⇒ exit 1) fires, and A's workaround ('CI=true is overridden to scope the fail-closed asserts') deliberately un-fails-closed the release acceptance test, so the harness never proves its rust gate runs on Linux. Fix (C's design): explicitly install rust + the pinned apt list + Swatinem/rust-cache in every linux job that runs validate, and let the gate run for real.

A puts tauri-specta bindings drift inside the 3–8s 'contracts' step: regenerating bindings requires executing the Rust exporter (a cargo build/test) — tens of seconds warm, minutes cold — and cannot share a step with openapi regen-diff. It must move to the stamp-gated rust step locally and the CI rust lane (fail closed there).

B's provenance DECISION regex includes JSON-only tokens ("csp", installMode): strict-JSON files (tauri.conf.json) cannot carry // SOURCE: comments, so posttool-source-check would block edits with no way to satisfy it. The provenance scanner must exclude .json files, and CSP/installer decisions are owned by check-tauri-policy + docs, not the citation gate.

B's ≤45s warm Stop budget encoded as a hard bar will be deleted, not met: 16 steps summing type-aware ESLint (5–15s) + tsc -b (3–8s) + knip (4–8s) + vite build (3–6s) + two cargo invocations + 8 script gates is ~30–60s best-case on the scaffold and blows past 45s on the first real feature — exactly the flaky-gate-teams-delete failure T7-P4 warns about. Budgets must be measured-then-asserted with headroom, never aspirational.

B's blanket write-guard PROTECTION of apps/desktop/src-tauri/Cargo.toml and capabilities/ breaks the authoring-vertical-slice skill it ships: adding a #[tauri::command] permission or a crate dependency is routine agent work; requiring HARNESS_ALLOW_SELF_EDIT=1 for it means humans export the variable permanently, disabling tamper evidence for the entire protected surface.

The windows tauri-build legs under-account for webviewInstallMode=offlineInstaller: bundling downloads the ~127MB WebView2 embed at build time (flaky on CI, slow), and A's `--no-bundle` variant proves compile but never exercises the NSIS/offlineInstaller path anywhere in the acceptance matrix. Fix: C's tauri-build-smoke lane (full bundle + /S install/uninstall + registry asserts) path-filtered + nightly, with the runtime download cached/retried; keep `--debug` builds elsewhere.

B's bootstrap matrix runs rustup + cargo warm-up + Postgres service + full validate in ONE job across a Node 22/24 matrix: cold-cache first runs are 15–25 min per leg and become the job every PR waits on; rust caches evict after 7 days so this recurs weekly. Split fast bootstrap legs from the rust-real leg and rely on the nightly warmer (A/C's shape).

C's tauri-isolation-as-module contradicts the check-tauri-policy gate as specified by the consensus: if the gate asserts pattern.use=='isolation' (A/B), C's scaffold fails bootstrap; if it doesn't, enabling the module later requires editing the write-guard-protected gate script. The scaffold and the policy assert must agree byte-for-byte on day one — resolved by shipping isolation default (contradiction #3) with the recorded relax-to-warn contingency.

B's coverage thresholds.autoUpdate 'on main after merge' requires CI committing to a protected branch — it conflicts with the branch ruleset that makes the quality gates required and with persist-credentials:false hygiene. The ratchet needs a bot-PR flow (or a scheduled ratchet PR); as written it is unimplementable without weakening branch protection.

Multiple regenerate-and-diff gates sit on rc/major-churn tools (drizzle-kit 1.0.0-rc migration layout, tauri-specta v2 rc bindings, @hono/zod-openapi/Zod-4 spec output): any unpinned bump flaps check-migrations/check-contract-drift repo-wide. All three note it, but the merged plan must make it structural: exact catalog pins (no ranges) for drizzle-kit/tauri-cli/tauri-specta/zod, Renovate minimumReleaseAge, and the selftest bootstrap matrix running on Renovate PRs (B's note) — otherwise bootstrap-green rots on the first dependabot-style bump.

harden-runner egress-policy: block is unsupported on Windows runners (audit-only) — any workflow or module that writes `block` on a windows job silently downgrades protection while claiming it. The workflow-lint rule must mechanically enforce audit-on-windows (and the direct gitleaks binary everywhere — gitleaks-action carries the license-key + Node20-sunset-2026-09-16 time bombs).

B's per-file REUSE/SPDX headers over template/** with installer header-stripping is a large v0.1 surface for zero functional gain: headers inside scaffolded sources must survive biome/hygiene/format gates and then be reliably stripped at init, and reuse-action becomes a required check on every new file. C's LICENSE-text 0BSD grant + the unit-tested installer LICENSE-writing step + an instantiation-CI grep for harness copyright strings achieves the legal effect (T12-P3) now; defer per-file REUSE to v0.2.
missing: pgvector extversion assert + iterative_scan doctrine (T5-r7/P6): all three digest-pin the pgvector/pg16 image, but none asserts `SELECT extversion FROM pg_extension` ≥ 0.8.2 (CVE-2026-3172) in the runtime catalog gate, and only C ships the dimension-contract demo column. Add both to the RLS catalog gate + scaffold (EMBEDDING_DIM constant asserted end-to-end).

HITL review-state machine as DB-enforced facts (T8-r7/P7): 'AI extraction with human-in-the-loop QA' is the consuming product's core, yet no design ships the pipeline-role-cannot-approve RLS/trigger test, the transition-matrix test, or the exemplar-store∩eval-holdout disjointness gate — B has it as doctrine-only. Add the reference design + tests to the eval/ops module and the vertical-slice skill references.

Confidence-calibration check (T8-P6): routing reviewer queues on raw model confidence without a bucketed accuracy-vs-confidence check silently mis-prioritizes review; no design mentions it. Add to eval-live module + doctrine.

GBNF/JSON-schema pre-validation gate (T8-r0/P0/P3): only C names it (inside eval-live). The merged plan must ship it explicitly — json_schema_to_grammar.py warnings + llama-gbnf-validator over every extraction schema — or constrained-decoding output is trusted beyond what the converter actually supports.

Zod single-instance gate (T6-P6/r9): all three pin zod via catalog, none ships the `pnpm why zod` resolves-to-exactly-one-version assert; two Zod instances break @hono/zod-openapi instanceof checks with incomprehensible errors. One-line script inside check-version-sync or check-contract-drift.

tsconfig-references sync (T2-r2): only A carries `workspaces-to-typescript-project-references --check`; B/C miss it. Three parallel dependency topologies (workspace deps, project references, knip map) will desynchronize into confusing type errors — wire the check into the contracts or lint step.

OpenTelemetry trace-correlated logging (T6-r6, T9-r6): every design stops at pino+redaction; no OTel SDK wiring, no span-per-route test, and no named module path — the recommendation is currently a dead end. Minimum: a named `observability` module (OTel NodeSDK + instrumentation-pino + InMemorySpanExporter route-coverage test) so it has a home.

Keyboard-registry WCAG 2.1.4 unit test (T7-r3 ENF): the registry ships in all three scaffolds, but nobody asserts 'no unmodified single-printable-character global shortcut' by iterating the registry — a trivial test that makes the keyboard-first AA claim non-vacuous from day one.

SVG-chart-library import ban as a DEFAULT lint (T7-r0/P0): A/B scope it to gate-perf-budget, but banning recharts/visx/d3-selection in matrix/graph feature globs via no-restricted-imports costs nothing at bootstrap and pre-empts the single most likely agent-generated perf mistake before the perf module exists.

Installer size budget (T0-r3 ENF): no design records or asserts installer size in the smoke/release lanes — the cheap detector for a silent downloadBootstrapper fallback (~0MB jump) or accidental fixedRuntime (~180MB jump). One assertion in tauri-build-smoke and ci-windows-release.

WebView2 ProcessFailed handling in the base Rust host (T10-r5/P5): B only tests kill/recover in the ci-windows-e2e module; no design registers add_ProcessFailed + a structured `webview_process_failed` log in the scaffold, so renderer crashes ship as invisible blank-window tickets by default. Ship the handler + log line in base; the kill/recover CI test stays module. Related base gaps A/C also miss that B has: tracing/tauri-plugin-log spine, dirty-shutdown sentinel, print-ban clippy lints — adopt B's base set.

pnpm cache-hit assertion + Windows store-path discipline (T4-P2): C picks pnpm/action-setup but nobody asserts cache-hit in a debug step — the classic 0%-hit-with-green-checkmarks failure. One-line assert in the selftest linux/windows jobs; also adopt the clippy disallowed-methods entry banning std::env::var("APPDATA") (T13-r5) — a one-line [workspace.lints] addition all three missed.
merged_recommendations:
## Merged design decisions (resolve every divergence)

### 1. Default gate chain (tools/harness.config.mjs)
Adopt B's gate CONTENT with C's execution mechanics, ordered cheap→expensive:
1. `format` — `pnpm exec biome ci .` (B/A's `ci` over C's `check`: read-only, CI-grade)
2. `rust-fmt` — `cargo fmt --check` via run-if (skip w/o cargo locally, forced in CI)
3. `types` — `pnpm exec tsc -b`
4. `lint` — `pnpm exec eslint . --max-warnings 0` (boundaries, jsx-a11y strict, cognitive-complexity error + warn-ratchet — NOT the sibling's max-lines caps; include the SVG-chart-lib ban and shortcut-registry rule by default)
5. `provenance` — check-sources.mjs (**.json files excluded**; regex lockstep with the hook; drop \"csp\"/installMode tokens)
6. `tauri-policy` — pure-JSON asserts incl. `pattern.use=="isolation"`, identity.lock match (A/B superset)
7. `version-sync` — root/tauri.conf/x-client-version + react-compiler exact pin + `pnpm why zod` single-instance + node-version (B, extended)
8. `prompts` — prompts.lock.json hash gate (B)
9. `licenses` — pnpm-native allowlist policy script (B; exceptions map is reviewable data)
10. `schema-rls` — pgTable↔pgPolicy manifest + rls-exempt.json (A/C)
11. `migrations` — drizzle drift + append-only vs HEAD + RLS-in-migration + no-DML + destructive-ADR coupling (B's superset)
12. `contracts` — openapi regen-diff (stable stringify) + `workspaces-to-typescript-project-references --check` (A). **No specta here.**
13. `dead-code` — `pnpm exec knip` (sibling parity `--strict` in CI profile)
14. `architecture` — depcruise law (desktop↛db/server, schema-only drizzle-zod, eval-adapters-only LLM endpoints)
15. `build` — vite build (desktop) + dist/ forbidden-marker grep (drizzle-orm, pg, TAURI_SIGNING, VITE_*KEY). No server tsc emit (tsc -b covers types).
16. `rust-check` — `cargo check --locked` behind C's **content-hash stamp** (.harness/rust-check.ok over src-tauri/** + Cargo.lock); stamp ignored + fail-closed when CI=true; specta bindings drift regen rides here (and in the CI rust lane), not in `contracts`.

STOP_HOOK_STEPS: `[validate] → [rls-isolation: node tests/rls/run-rls.mjs] → [unit: vitest --project unit-node --project unit-dom]` — verbatim doctrine, skip-local/fail-closed-CI, seeded positive controls, 42501 asserts, GUC leak test (pool max=1), **pgvector extversion ≥ 0.8.2 catalog assert added**. Stop hook timeout stays 600 (verified in sibling). Latency: no aspirational number — selftest measures warm wall time and asserts ≤120s with warn at 90.

FLOOR = all 16 step names/commands verbatim; shape-awareness lives INSIDE gate scripts (loud SKIP when the surface is absent, fail closed in CI when present), not in floor membership. Selftest asserts FLOOR↔VALIDATE_STEPS name+command lockstep (A).

### 2. Defaults vs modules
**Default in the scaffold**: isolation pattern (with A's ADR'd relax-to-warn contingency), tauri-specta committed bindings + src/ipc-only rule, React Compiler exact-pinned, keyboard registry **with the 2.1.4 registry-iterating unit test**, connection-status w/ zod shape probe, SSE demo route + abort-propagation test (in-process @hono/node-server, no testcontainers), skew middleware + route-coverage test, EMBEDDING_DIM + vector demo column + dimension test, provenance-columns example table (B), tracing/tauri-plugin-log spine + print bans + dirty-shutdown sentinel + ProcessFailed handler stub + build.rs longPathAware + APPDATA disallowed-methods lint (B base, extended).
**Merged module catalog (12)**: ci-windows-release (hand-rolled DAG: sign → signtool verify /pa /all → NSIS /S smoke → Defender -DisableRemediation → determinism spot-check → checksums/latest.json → release-please; **+ installer size budget**), ci-windows-e2e (WDIO embedded provider + **absorbs ci-enterprise-net**: mitmproxy TLS smoke, redirected-APPDATA, >280-char paths, ProcessFailed kill/recover), ci-macos, mutation (Stryker incremental + cargo-mutants --in-diff), ci-provenance (SBOM both ecosystems + attest+verify + NOTICES drift), gate-perf-budget (absorbs ci-perf; pinned-runner median-of-N), gate-a11y-deep (route-manifest axe wcag22aa + keyboard traversal + NVDA checklist; **absorbs gate-screen-registry** intent), gate-styleguide (OKLCH, all three escape channels), crash-reporting (self-hosted Sentry + PDB gates + diagnostics bundle + redaction test), ops-backup (pgBackRest drills + backfill runner + **HITL state-machine tests**), updater-flavor, eval-live (GPU runner, **+ GBNF pre-validation + exemplar/holdout disjointness + confidence calibration**), observability (NEW: OTel + span-per-route test). Drop: gate-pgtap (plain-SQL catalog gate suffices), ZAP (no public routes; keep semgrep inside rust-security/codeql surface or a tiny ci-sast). Tiers: core=[], standard=[ci-provenance, ci-windows-release], strict=all.

### 3. Hooks
hookio verbatim + **B's fail-loud uncaughtException/unhandledRejection handlers, unit-tested with malformed stdin** (T12-P2). Bash-guard = union table incl. `git commit --no-verify` (B-only), drizzle-kit push, knip --fix, cargo/pnpm update, signing-material patterns, migrator/postgres DSN probes — with A's context-scoping (DROP only in psql/one-liner contexts) to bound false positives. Write-guard: content-checks (not blanket protection) for tauri.conf/capabilities/Cargo.toml; blanket PROTECTED for gate scripts, configs, workflows, identity.lock.json, prompts.lock, knip.json, pnpm-workspace.yaml; **append-only migrations rule** (B/C); GUC discipline (SET LOCAL only, set_config third-arg true); VITE_ secret names; vitest.workspace/defineWorkspace ban; WITH RECURSIVE without CYCLE/visited; positive DAL-imports-context requirement (A). `as any`/@ts-ignore stay ESLint-owned. HARNESS_HOOK_VERSION stamps + doctor stale-vs-modified probe (A/B).

### 4. Auth
`AUTH_MODE=entra|stub` (C's naming), byte-identical verification path (jose, iss/aud/tid/alg pinning, clockTolerance 300, ±4/±6-min tests), local JWKS minter for bootstrap, **shipped boot-time fatal when NODE_ENV=production && AUTH_MODE=stub with a unit test** (C), hygiene leak pattern for the stub key (A). Desktop: StubAuthProvider + documented PKCE/system-browser skeleton (C's honest cut). Entra IDs = runtime env in env.example, NOT placeholders; API_ORIGIN IS a placeholder (feeds committed CSP).

### 5. Installer/retrofit
detect: bootstrap=empty dir; retrofit=pnpm-workspace.yaml + tauri/hono signature; hard-reject next/npm/yarn/src-single-root with guidance (no --force-map). Merge: root package.json (never-clobber, harness:*) + merge-workspace-yaml.mjs (glob union, catalog add-missing/never-downgrade); workspace packages additive-only; per-workspace merging deferred, documented. Placeholders: 5 carried + PRODUCT_IDENTIFIER (≤30-char lint) + WINDOWS_PUBLISHER + API_ORIGIN + DB_NAME, declaration order preserved. License: Apache-2.0 + explicit 0BSD grant for template/** in LICENSE text, installer writes consumer LICENSE (unit-tested) + instantiation-CI copyright grep; per-file REUSE deferred to v0.2. AGENTS.md canonical, CLAUDE.md='@AGENTS.md', CI equality + docs-commands-verify job.

### 6. CI
**Harness selftest**: installer-unit; hook-contracts (incl. malformed-stdin fail-loud); canary (both directions + injection-landed grep); bootstrap-linux (node 22/24: **pinned webkit apt list + explicit rustup + Swatinem/rust-cache + pnpm/action-setup with cache-hit assert**; validate green with rust running for real; RLS fail-closed grep); bootstrap-windows **every PR** (validate + vitest); tauri-build-smoke (path-filtered + nightly: full debug NSIS bundle, /S install → Program Files + HKLM asserts → uninstall clean, **size budget**, cached WebView2 embed); retrofit non-clobber; runtime-postgres (pgvector service; '[rls] OK' + catalog asserts must actually run); placeholder-residue + dotless-rename asserts; selftest also runs on Renovate PRs. **Shipped template CI**: quality-gate (min-floor w/ HARNESS_REQUIRE_TOOLCHAINS, unit, runtime-RLS, rust lane [clippy -D warnings, nextest, capabilities-vs-fresh-schema ajv, cargo-deny], Playwright fast lane [mock-IPC + inventory-vs-#[tauri::command] diff + axe wcag22aa + degraded-network], desktop-windows path-filtered + nightly); migration-safety (squawk + ADR coupling + append-only); api-contract (regen-diff + oasdiff-vs-main; skew-n1 self-skips until first release); adr-guard (B); coverage ratchet via bot-PR; codeql (js-ts + actions; rust via cargo-audit/deny), osv both lockfiles, gitleaks binary direct, actionlint+zizmor (audit-on-windows rule enforced), all SHA-pinned + harden-runner. Exact catalog pins (drizzle-kit, tauri-cli, tauri-specta, zod, typescript 6.0.x) + Renovate cooldown.

### 7. Sequence & cut line
Follow C's dependency-ordered build (skeleton → hooks-stack-agnostic-first → tooling core → schema → server → desktop → gates-one-at-a-time-staying-green → RLS runtime → eval/importer → hook finalization → CI → modules → docs → dress rehearsal), with B's anti-vacuity pass (inject a violation per gate once, record in gates-catalog) and A's release checklist (npm-pack dotless survival, plugin marketplace, template flag). Every deferral has a named module (C's rule); the deferred list at v0.1: per-workspace retrofit merge, REUSE per-file, browser-mode default lane, Sentry, pgBackRest, N-1 skew activation, Stage-2 pgvector corpus search.
